column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-accessibility (a11y-01 … a11y-12).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard → runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and decides. Structural rules that
# grep cannot express (Chart-without-descriptor) live in ast-grep/*.yml (tier 2). a11y-01 (icon-only control)
# stays TIER 1 by design: a correct .accessibilityLabel chains OUTSIDE the Button call node, so a containment
# rule would false-positive — the LLM READ step (SKILL.md step 3) confirms the label's absence instead.

column4: INVENTED accessibility API (does not exist) → .accessibilityLabel()/.accessibilityHint() (a11y-10, fix_mode: auto)

icon-only tab/toolbar item — confirm an icon-only Image item carries .accessibilityShowsLargeContentViewer (iOS 15+) so it stays readable under the large-content viewer at AX text sizes (a11y-12). NOTE: AccessibilityTraits.isToggle is iOS 17.0 = the project floor, so it needs NO gate (flag a missing toggle trait as a11y-08, not a11y-12). a11y-12's gating arm fires only for an iOS-18+ a11y API used ungated.

LEGACY combined .accessibility(label:/hint:/addTraits:/value:) modifier → split into .accessibilityLabel()/.accessibilityHint()/.accessibilityAddTraits()/.accessibilityValue() (a11y-11, fix_mode: auto)

icon-only control candidate — if this Image/Label is the sole content of a Button/tappable with NO .accessibilityLabel, VoiceOver reads the SF Symbol name or nothing (a11y-01). Confirm a label exists.

Chart/Canvas — confirm an a11y representation exists: .accessibilityChartDescriptor / .accessibilityRepresentation / .accessibilityLabel+.accessibilityValue (a11y-07). cross_ref charts / drawing-canvas.
value control — a CUSTOM (hand-rolled) gauge/slider/progress must carry .accessibilityValue(); native controls expose it automatically (a11y-04). READ to confirm it is custom.

custom gesture (tap/long-press/DragGesture/swipe) on a non-Button — VoiceOver sees no actionable trait AND cannot perform the gesture; add .accessibilityAddTraits(.isButton) (or .isToggle) and a mirrored .accessibilityAction (a11y-08). cross_ref touch-gestures.
status-by-color — if color is the ONLY cue (no label/shape/symbol), it fails Differentiate-Without-Color (a11y-05). cross_ref appearance-color.

motion — if there is no @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) branch disabling/replacing it, the animation ignores Reduce Motion (a11y-06). cross_ref animation-motion (motion construction is theirs; "ignores the flag" is ours).

grouping present — VERIFY children:.combine/.ignore is correct; ABSENCE on a custom composite (HStack/VStack of Text+Image read as one control) is the real defect (a11y-03) — locate composites by reading.
VoiceOver focus — an AccessibilityFocusState declared-but-never-driven, or a broken reading order, leaves focus stranded (a11y-09).
decorative image — a purely-decorative Image with no informational value should be .accessibilityHidden(true) so VoiceOver skips it (a11y-02). READ to confirm it is decorative.
